title: ConfigurablePropBase
description: A configuration or input field for a component.

name.description: When building `configuredProps`, make sure to use this field as the key when setting the prop value

label.description: Value to use as an input label. In cases where `type` is "app", should load the app via `getApp`, etc. and show `app.name` instead.

description.description: A description of the prop, shown to the user when configuring the component.

optional.description: If true, this prop does not need to be specified.

disabled.description: If true, this prop will be ignored.

readOnly.description: If true, this prop is read-only — its value is either fixed by the component author (`static`) or the prop is purely informational (e.g. `alert`, `dir`). Connect clients should render it without treating it as a configurable input.

hidden.description: If true, should not expose this prop to the user

remoteOptions.description: If true, call `configureComponent` for this prop to load remote options. It is safe, and preferred, given a returned list of { label: string; value: any } objects to set the prop value to { __lv: { label: string; value: any } }. This way, on load, you can access label for the value without necessarily reloading these options

useQuery.description: If true, calls to `configureComponent` for this prop support receiving a `query` parameter to filter remote options

reloadProps.description: If true, after setting a value for this prop, a call to `reloadComponentProps` is required as the component has dynamic configurable props dependent on this one

withLabel.description: If true, you must save the configured prop value as a "label-value" object which should look like: { __lv: { label: string; value: any } } because the execution needs to access the label
